title: [FIRRTL] InferDomains: Add debug logging
body:
Add debug logging, change the printed format of "rows of domains" to `[x : ClockDomain, b : PowerDomain]`, updating the tests accordingly.

An example of the logging:
```
firrtl.circuit "NodeTest" {
  firrtl.domain @ClockDomain
  // CHECK: firrtl.module @NodeTest(in %ClockDomain: !firrtl.domain<@ClockDomain()>, in %i: !firrtl.uint<1> domains [%ClockDomain], out %o: !firrtl.uint<1> domains [%ClockDomain]) {
  // CHECK:   %n = firrtl.node %i : !firrtl.uint<1>
  // CHECK:   firrtl.matchingconnect %o, %n : !firrtl.uint<1>
  // CHECK: }
  firrtl.module @NodeTest(in %i: !firrtl.uint<1>, out %o: !firrtl.uint<1>) {
    %n = firrtl.node %i : !firrtl.uint<1>
    firrtl.matchingconnect %o, %n : !firrtl.uint<1>
  }
}
```

```
infer: NodeTest
  processing:
    process port i
      set domains(i) := [?1]
    process port o
      set domains(o) := [?2]
    process %n = firrtl.node %i : !firrtl.uint<1>
      unify domains(i) = domains(n)
      set domains(n) := [?1]
    process firrtl.matchingconnect %o, %n : !firrtl.uint<1>
      unify domains(o) = domains(n)
      unify [?2] = [?1]
      unify ?2 = ?1
  domain exports:
  applying updates:
    new-input ClockDomain : ClockDomain
      unify ?1 = ClockDomain : ClockDomain
  port summary:
    ClockDomain : ClockDomain
    i domains [ClockDomain : ClockDomain]
    o domains [ClockDomain : ClockDomain]
```
